question: What type of reactor is the NuScale NSSS?

nuscale_ch01_0188.title: BTP 6-1, (March 2007): pH for Emergency Coolant Water for Pressurized Water ReactorsB.3 Hydrogen Generation from Aluminum Corrosion
nuscale_ch01_0188.summary: The section reviews NuScale's conformance with regulatory criteria regarding pH control and hydrogen generation from aluminum corrosion in emergency coolant water. While NuScale's design lacks certain systems referenced in the criteria (such as containment spray systems and sumps), the intent of the requirements is still met due to the collection and recirculation of ECCS water within the containment vessel. Other criteria related to minimum containment pressure and bypass leakage paths are deemed not applicable to the NuScale design.

nuscale_ch01_0018.title: Principal Design Criteria
nuscale_ch01_0018.summary: The Principal Design Criteria section describes the NuScale reactor's core cooling and power generation process, emphasizing the role of the Site Cooling Water System (SCWS) in supporting auxiliary systems. It outlines the natural circulation of reactor coolant for heat transfer, the generation of superheated steam to drive a turbine and generator, and the use of a circulating water loop to remove heat via the condenser.

nuscale_ch01_0186.title: BTP 6-1, (March 2007): pH for Emergency Coolant Water for Pressurized Water ReactorsB.1 Minimum pH for Emergency Coolant Water
nuscale_ch01_0186.summary: This section discusses NuScale’s conformance with regulatory criteria regarding the minimum pH for emergency coolant water in pressurized water reactors. While certain requirements in BTP 6-1 reference systems not present in the NuScale design (such as a containment spray system or sump), the intent of the acceptance criterion is still applicable because ECCS water is recirculated within the containment vessel. Thus, NuScale’s design aligns with the regulatory intent for emergency coolant water pH control.

nuscale_ch01_0005.title: Reactor Type
nuscale_ch01_0005.summary: The NuScale reactor is a small modular pressurized water reactor (PWR) featuring a cylindrical steel containment vessel designed to ASME standards, with internal pressure maintained at a vacuum during normal operation. Each NuScale Power Module (NPM) integrates the reactor core, steam generators, and pressurizer within a single vessel, uses natural circulation for cooling (eliminating the need for pumps), and is rated at 160 MWt (about 50 MWe), with up to 12 modules per plant for a total net output of approximately 570 MWe.

nuscale_ch01_0019.title: Operating Characteristics
nuscale_ch01_0019.summary: The NuScale Power Module (NPM) operates using natural circulation for reactor coolant flow, eliminating the need for reactor coolant pumps, and features a small, modular, integral pressurized water reactor design. It relies on passive safety systems—including dedicated emergency core cooling and decay heat removal systems—that do not require electrical power, and its components are housed in steel containment partially immersed in water for effective heat removal. The NPM allows for flexible power maneuvering using control rods and soluble boron, and its modular construction enables in-shop fabrication of major components.

nuscale_ch01_0004.title: Containment Type
nuscale_ch01_0004.summary: The NuScale containment vessel is a cylindrical, steel, ASME-certified pressure vessel designed to withstand high-pressure events and operates under vacuum during normal conditions, eliminating the need for insulation. The reactor is a passive, integral small modular pressurized water reactor (SMR) with natural circulation and no external piping for steam generators or pressurizer, and each module produces about 50 MWe, with a 12-module plant yielding a net output of approximately 570 MWe.

Section B describes the NuScale containment vessel as a cylindrical, steel, ASME BPVC Class MC containment designed for high-pressure transients, which supports the classification of the NuScale NSSS as a pressurized water reactor referenced in Section A.

Section A discusses regulatory conformance for pressurized water reactors (PWRs), while Section B describes the NuScale containment vessel as designed for a PWR, thus Section B provides design details that specify the reactor type referenced in Section A.
